description_long: This is a headless marble statue of a draped human figure, likely representing a classical or Greco-Roman subject. The figure is clothed in intricately carved, flowing robes that cascade down to the feet, with detailed folds and creases that suggest a heavy, layered garment. The arms are partially missing, and the statue stands on a rectangular plinth, which appears to be made of the same material as the figure. The back of the statue shows the drapery gathered and falling in naturalistic folds, while the sides reveal the thickness and depth of the garment. The surface is predominantly white with subtle variations in tone, and the overall form is solid and monolithic. There are no visible inscriptions, plaques, or artist signatures on the base or nearby.
description: A headless, draped marble statue of a human figure with detailed flowing robes, standing on a rectangular plinth.
description_view_0: Front view showing the detailed drapery and the missing head and arms, with the folds of the robe prominently displayed.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and layering of the robe, as well as the thickness of the statue and the plinth.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the gathered drapery and the naturalistic flow of the garment down the back.
description_view_3: Angled rear-side view emphasizing the curvature of the robe and the solid stance of the figure on the plinth.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
